text: A custom_component simply renders the markup you give it.

content.html:
Hello from a custom component

The custom_component block mounts arbitrary author-provided HTML or SVG inside an evaluation, so you can drop in bespoke visualizations without writing a new block type.

text: With zoomable=True the component wraps raw SVG so it gets mouse-wheel zoom and click-drag pan for free.

content.html:
<svg viewBox="0 0 400 300" width="400" height="300" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     style="display:block;margin:0 auto;background:#fafafa;">
  <circle cx="120" cy="150" r="70" fill="#fca5a5" stroke="#b91c1c" stroke-width="2"/>
  <rect x="210" y="90" width="120" height="120" rx="10" fill="#93c5fd" stroke="#1d4ed8" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="200" y="285" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Arial" font-size="14" fill="#555">scroll to zoom, drag to pan</text>
</svg>

text: htmleval stays generic: it only inlines the element JS via addCustomElement. The element interacts with the eval through the window.htmleval signal API (emit / subscribe).

content.html: These <demo-badge> custom elements were registered with addCustomElement. Click one to emit its signal; every badge receives signals and highlights when its own is active.

text: Clicking here emits alpha — watch the ALPHA badge highlight (if not already selected).
